skill.name: go-playwright
skill.description: Guidance for building browser automation and testing workflows with Playwright Go.
skill.summary: Build Playwright Go scripts for browser testing, scraping, logging, and session handling.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static code-execution, weak-crypto, keylogger, and hardcoded-IP alerts are mostly false positives from Markdown formatting, Go import examples, viewport dimensions, and documentation links. However, the skill intentionally instructs agents to bypass anti-bot systems with human-like typing, mouse movement, user-agent rotation, behavioral noise, and Cloudflare or Akamai evasion. This is not confirmed malware, but it is high risk for a public marketplace.

high_findings.title: Anti-Bot Evasion Guidance
high_findings.description: The skill tells agents to bypass anti-bot systems and generate human-like behavior using typing delays, mouse movement, viewport randomization, behavioral noise, and user-agent rotation. This can enable abusive scraping or account automation against protected services.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious behavior is an explicit intended capability, not only a keyword match. Multiple sections describe bypassing Cloudflare or Akamai and implementing human-like automation.

medium_findings.title: Cookie Session Persistence Guidance
medium_findings.description: The playbook includes SaveSession and LoadSession functions for browser cookies. The code is mostly comments, but saving cookies to arbitrary file paths can expose authenticated sessions if implemented without encryption, access controls, or user consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file shows session-cookie persistence semantics, but it does not include a full working implementation or exfiltration path. The risk depends on how generated code writes and protects those files.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Website Automation
medium_findings.description: The skill targets scraping, automation, and testing of websites with dynamic content. This is a legitimate testing capability, but it can also automate requests and interactions against external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network-facing purpose is directly stated and backed by an example navigation target. Legitimate uses are plausible, so this is not classified as malicious by itself.

low_findings.title: Static Code Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The dynamic import and Ruby backtick alerts point to Go import blocks, Markdown code fences, or inline code examples. I did not find evidence of executable Ruby backticks or dynamic runtime imports in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown documentation or Go examples. No standalone script or command execution instruction is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: Static Hardcoded IP And Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The hardcoded IP alert is a browser viewport size, and the weak-crypto alerts are triggered by words or identifiers in Markdown text. I did not find evidence of cryptographic logic or hardcoded infrastructure endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context shows viewport dimensions, metadata, and prose. There is no hash function, encryption routine, or hardcoded IP address used for connectivity.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: The reviewed files do not contain instructions that impersonate system messages, override the evaluator, claim pre-approval, or tell the auditor to skip security analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both files were reviewed in full. No text matching the prompt-injection patterns from the audit instructions was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Stealth Automation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs generated code to imitate users and avoid bot detection through movement, typing, scrolling, focus changes, and user-agent rotation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is described as a required generation behavior and is supported by concrete helper examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Cookie File Persistence
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes saving and loading browser cookies from a filepath, which can preserve authenticated sessions outside the browser context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code is incomplete, but the function names and comments clearly describe browser cookie serialization and loading.

content.value_statement: Browser automation in Go can be hard to structure, observe, and debug. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for Playwright Go scripts with logging, contexts, timeouts, and session patterns.
actual_capabilities: Generate Playwright Go setup patterns with browser launch, context creation, page creation, and cleanup.
Recommend structured logging with Zap for navigation, clicks, inputs, and error handling.
Guide explicit timeout use and panic recovery for more stable automation routines.
Show interaction helper patterns for typing, clicking, scrolling, and browser debugging.
Describe browser session save and load concepts for cookie-based workflows.
limitations: Requires Playwright Go drivers and browsers to be installed before scripts can run.
Full browser automation can use significant CPU and memory on local or CI machines.
Does not include CAPTCHA solving or guaranteed access through strict bot defenses.
Contains anti-bot evasion guidance, so marketplace use requires policy review.

use_cases.title: Create Browser Regression Tests
use_cases.description: Generate Go automation flows for pages that require a real browser, selectors, timeouts, and structured logs.

use_cases.title: Debug Existing Playwright Scripts
use_cases.description: Review flaky Playwright Go scripts and improve context reuse, cleanup, logging, and wait behavior.

use_cases.title: Prototype Data Collection Workflows
use_cases.description: Draft browser-based collection scripts for dynamic pages when simple HTTP requests cannot render the content.

prompt_templates.title: Start A Playwright Go Script
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new automation task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Playwright Go script that opens a browser, creates one isolated context, visits my target page, logs each major step with Zap, and closes resources cleanly.

prompt_templates.title: Add Reliable Interaction Handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a script with better waits and error handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Playwright Go flow and add explicit timeouts, locator-based actions, structured logging, and graceful cleanup for pages, contexts, and the browser.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Multi-Session Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan several independent browser sessions in one process.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Playwright Go architecture that launches one browser and creates separate contexts for independent sessions, with clear lifecycle management and logging.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Automation Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before using automation on external sites.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this Playwright Go automation plan for reliability, security, session-data handling, and acceptable-use risks before implementation.

output_examples.input: Build a Playwright Go script for a logged browser flow.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan with setup steps, context lifecycle guidance, logging points, timeout choices, and session-storage cautions.

output_examples.input: My Playwright Go script is flaky in CI.
output_examples.output: A debugging checklist covering browser installation, headless settings, explicit waits, selector stability, cleanup, and diagnostic logging.

output_examples.input: Review this automation before I run it on a public site.
output_examples.output: A risk review that separates legitimate testing needs from scraping, rate-limit, authentication, and anti-bot policy concerns.

best_practices: Use one browser process with separate contexts for isolated sessions.
Log navigation, input, click, and error events with structured Zap fields.
Handle cookies and session files as sensitive data with explicit user approval.
anti_patterns: Do not launch a separate browser process for every small task.
Do not store authentication cookies in shared or unprotected paths.
Do not use automation to bypass access controls, rate limits, or site terms.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps generate and review Playwright Go browser automation scripts for testing, debugging, and dynamic-page workflows.

faq.question: Does it run Playwright by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and code patterns. The local environment still needs Go, Playwright Go, and browser dependencies.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The skill explicitly includes anti-bot evasion guidance for protected services, including Cloudflare and Akamai.

faq.question: Can it be used for legitimate testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can support authorized browser tests and debugging when used on owned systems or approved targets.

faq.question: Does it include CAPTCHA solving?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that it does not include built-in CAPTCHA solving capabilities.

faq.question: How should session data be handled?
faq.answer: Treat cookies and saved browser sessions as secrets. Store them only with user approval and strong access controls.
